LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY'S, MOTION FOR DISCHARGE FROM INTERPLEADER, LIABILITY AND FOR AN AWARD OF FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. ACIC IS DISCHARGED FROM ANY AND ALL CLAIMS AND LIABILITY OF ANY KIND FROM THE DEFENDANTS IN THIS CASE AS TO THE APPEAL BOND. ACIC IS AWARDED $30,860.64 FROM THE INTERPLEADED FUNDS FOR THE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IT REASONABLY INCURRED IN THIS INTERPLEADER ACTION RELATING TO THE APPEAL BOND. ORDER SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discharge From Interpleader By American Contractors Indemnity Company
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY's, Motion For Discharge from Interpleader, liability and for an award of fees and costs is granted. ACIC is discharged from any and all claims and liability of any kind from the defendants in this case as to the Appeal Bond. ACIC is awarded $30,860.64 from the interpleaded funds for the attorney's fees and costs it reasonably incurred in this interpleader action relating to the Appeal Bond. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Mar-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Geoff Wiggs, of Law Offices of Geoffrey Wiggs, Tel. 650.577.5952, for defendants Stinson & Fossat;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHARLES STINSON, MADELYN FOSSAT's Motion For Relief from the October 24, 2016, entry of their default on the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant Phyllis Voisenat is granted. Mr. Stinson and Ms. Fossat have shown excusable neglect per CCP 437(b). A fair reading of the cross-complaint discloses that, although they are named as cross-defendants, no relief is sought against them and thus there would be no need for them to respond to the complaint. Moreover, it was reasonable for them to assume that the cross-complaint had ceased to have any effect as to any cross-defendant as a result of the July 29, 2016 order on ACIC's demurrer to the cross-complaint. Based on that demurrer, the court should not have issued the August 29, 2016 order to show cause, which appears to have precipitated the request to enter the default of Mr. Stinson and Ms. Fossat. In all events, given the recent statement of decision by Judge Lee in case 534098 it would be extremely inequitable to allow the entry of default to stand. Ms. Voisenat's request for fees and costs is denied. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-01-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Shashauna Szczechowicz of Wolkin Curran LLP (415-982-9390) appears telephonically for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party, American Contractors Indemnity Company

Attorney, Marc Voisenat, Esq. (510-263-8664) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party, Phyllis Voisenat

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification as set forth on the record ans below.

CROSS-DEFENDANT AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. There are two reasons why both cross-claims fail as a matter of law. First, such cross-claims are impermissible in a lawsuit initiated by interpleader. Second, there is no allegation, nor does it appear that there could reasonably be such an allegation, that ACIC has any liability other than the bonds it issued and as a matter of law it no longer has any liability on the bonds. The defendants in an interpleader cannot obtain affirmative relief against the plaintiff. (Conner v. Bank of Bakersfield (1920) 183 Cal. 199, 203-204; State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. v. Pietak (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 600, 612-13). Any claims by the defendant against the interpleading plaintiff arising out of the subject matter of the interpleader must be raised in another and different action. (Id.) "As against the stakeholder, claimants may raise only matters which go to whether . . . the elements of an interpleader action are present." (State Farm at 612; see also Los Angeles v. Amidor (1903) 140 Cal. 400, 401 (In an interpleader action, "a defendant cannot, by a counterclaim or cross-complaint, change the character of the action. [Citation.] The only relief which a defendant can have against the plaintiff in such a suit is to have the action dismissed.").) The cases Ms. Voisenat relies on in her opposition are unavailing as "the propriety of including such claims in an interpleader action was not raised. Cases are not authority for propositions not considered therein." (State Farm at 614.). By interpleading the amount of the appeal bond, ACIC has extinguished its liability on that bond. ACIC has no liability on the injunction bond since the appellate decision reversed the damages award and there has been no subsequent damages that are covered by the bond. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

Off Calendar Apr-11-2016 Continued To Jun-08-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PHYLLIS VOISENAT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled to both causes of action in the complaint. Both claims are adequately pled. There is nothing impermissible for a plaintiff to assert no right to and to interplead as to one bond and assert a right to and not to interplead as to another bond. The issues raised by the complaint are not identical to the issues in the other action. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)
